title: StudioDash Integrations
description: StudioDash integration coverage for App Store Connect, AdMob, AdSense, Cloudflare, Google Search Console, Google Ads, and GitHub.
summary: StudioDash brings provider signals into one workspace while clearly separating implemented coverage from placeholder and roadmap integrations.

sections.heading: Integration approach
paragraphs: StudioDash is designed to connect the services indie developers already use to run apps, games, websites, advertising, and source projects.

The app distinguishes provider health, setup state, sync status, and available data so users can tell the difference between a connected provider, a provider needing attention, a provider with no data yet, and a planned integration.

sections.heading: Documented integrations
paragraphs: Current public documentation covers the providers below. Integration status wording is intentionally conservative while StudioDash remains in development.
bullets: App Store Connect: app metadata coverage is documented, with sales and analytics reporting described as in development where applicable.
AdMob: OAuth-backed advertising reporting and inventory coverage are documented.
AdSense: documented as placeholder coverage for website monetisation, not as a current live reporting client.
Cloudflare: traffic, website analytics, provider health, and Worker-related coverage are documented.
Google Search Console: OAuth-backed search visibility and indexing coverage are documented.
Google Ads: documented as placeholder coverage for campaign metrics.
GitHub: documented as placeholder coverage for repository activity.

sections.heading: Roadmap integrations
paragraphs: Additional services may be added as StudioDash evolves. Future integrations should be treated as roadmap content until they are implemented, tested, and represented in the product.
The goal is not to replace every provider dashboard. StudioDash focuses on the cross-provider signals developers need to review frequently.
